description: Cord, thread, wire, or similar strands form the flexible open mesh called 'mera'. Its joined openings can hold or carry things, support them, or separate material by size while air and water pass. 'niro' (web) emphasizes an interconnected structure, and 'phaliso' (network) the relations among connected points.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and open into stressed mid-front 'e'. Tap the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then lower the jaw into open 'a'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Hand-tied mesh can carry produce, support climbing plants, dry food, or sort material while remaining easy to inspect and mend.

semantic_domains.creation: a made mesh whose joined strands leave regular openings
semantic_domains.physical: a flexible open structure used to hold, carry, support, separate, or filter
